eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.

enure: verb. To inure; to make accustomed or desensitized to something unpleasant due to constant exposure.

eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.

gene: noun. A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of
generous: adjective. Noble in behaviour or actions; principled, not petty; kind, magnanimous.

genro: noun. A body of elder statesmen of Japan, formerly used as informal advisors to the Emperor.
gens: noun. A legally defined unit of Roman society, being a collection of people related through a common ancestor by birth, marriage or
genus: noun. A category in the classification of organisms, ranking below family (Lat. familia) and above species.

goer: noun. One who, or that which, goes.
goes: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of go

gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.

gree: noun. One of a flight of steps.
green: noun. The color of grass and leaves; a primary additive color midway between yellow and blue which is evoked by light between roughly
greens: noun. Leaves and leaf-like parts of edible plants when eaten as vegetables or in salads.

grue: verb. To be frightened; also, to shudder with fear; to quake, to tremble.

ours: pronoun. That or those belonging to us.

run: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kirundi.
rune: noun. A letter, or character, used in the written language of various ancient Germanic peoples, especially the Scandinavians and the
rung: noun. A crosspiece forming a step of a ladder; a round.

sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels

sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
